summary: Review Logic Apps for workflow definition, trigger, downstream action, connector, and identity posture that can repurpose trusted automation.

targets.takeover_reason: external trigger, downstream action posture, and workflow identity are visible; current identity has visible Logic App workflow write control

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Keeps the existing automation resource, trigger path, and operational context in place.
capability_steps.boundary: Workflow posture does not prove the workflow ran.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can add, remove, or repurpose actions while the trusted Logic App remains the same resource.
capability_steps.boundary: Default output does not print full workflow definition bodies.

capability_steps.api_surface: request, recurrence, api-connection, or event trigger
capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can keep an existing inbound, scheduled, or connector trigger while changing what happens after it fires.
capability_steps.boundary: Trigger posture does not prove trigger invocation.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can run altered workflow logic through already-integrated connectors or managed identity context.
capability_steps.boundary: Connector credential values and secret material are not collected.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Normal cover stories include connector refresh, integration repair, retry handling, or workflow modernization.
capability_steps.boundary: Cover story is not an intent claim.
